headline: Malaysia-Philippines Security Pact Targets Abu Sayyaf
context: Malaysia and Philippines signed enhanced security cooperation in February 2026 targeting Abu Sayyaf in Sulu-Celebes Sea.

cards.big: Malaysia-Philippines pact targets Abu Sayyaf in Sulu-Celebes region
cards.sub: Agreement enables hot pursuit across maritime boundaries for first time

cards.big: Hot pursuit allows forces to cross 50km into partner territory
cards.sub: Previously forces stopped at boundaries allowing suspects to escape

cards.big: Joint command centre in Tawau coordinates 24-hour surveillance
cards.sub: Real-time sharing replaces previous 48-hour information exchange delays

cards.big: Abu Sayyaf conducted 8 kidnapping operations in border region in 2025
cards.sub: Ransom payments totalling RM45 million funded continued operations

cards.big: Hot pursuit provisions test whether bilateral security cooperation can eliminate cross-border threats
Philippines security pact targeting Abu Sayyaf. Joint operations work when intelligence sharing is genuine.
